title: Aulacospermum multifidum
taxonomy: Aulacospermum multifidum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Aulacospermum, which consists of several species of plants found in the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The exact classification of Aulacospermum multifidum can be further detailed as follows: it is a member of the family Apiaceae, subfamily Apioideae, and tribe Apieae. Aulacospermum multifidum's classification is also notable as it is considered to be one of the many rare and endemic species found in the Himalayan region. Further research is required to fully understand its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Aulacospermum multifidum, commonly known as Himalayan angelica, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Aulacospermum multifidum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory issues in traditional medicine practices.
